name: Don Redman
abstract: Donald Matthew Redman (July 29, 1900 – November 30, 1964) was an American jazz musician, arranger, bandleader, and composer.

works_created.t: Gee Baby, Ain't I Good To You

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: So he had Don Redman put together a band... So we went on this tour... Don Redman rearranged some of the material that we had been playing and wrote some new material for that instrumentation and so we had quite a successful engagement.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard R. Scott
about_quotes.evidence: I was with a band... That Coleman Hawkins... And Don Redman... But the thing was, Fletcher was an arranger. He arranged all the music we played.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: So then after that I also played at the Savoy with Don Redman. We made records. We made a lot of records while I was in that band.

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: I already had my job with Don Redman. So I went with Don, and I had a great experience with Don. That was great to work with him

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: Well, no. Both of them, Don -- in fact, I later on played with Don in another band, but I couldn't see leaving California.

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: And a cab stopped and we were on the side of Lafayette and a little guy got out and Zutty said, there's Don Redman.
